folio.label: 76v
folio.l: A Frere wyl entermente hi~ , euäe moo
Loo goodmen , a flye and eke a Frere
Wol falle in euäy dissú , and eke matere
What spekest thow , of preambulaciouö
What aumble or trotte , or pes , or go sitte dounö
Thow lettest oure disport , in this manere
¢ ñe wilt æè so Sompnour , q¶ the Frere
Now be my feitú , I shal er that I goo
Telle of Sompnoures , swicú a tale or twoo
That alle folk , shuln laugúen , in this place
Now elles Frere , I be shrewe thyn face
Quod the Sompnour , and I be shrewe me
But if I telle tales , two or three
Of Freres , or æ I come to Sydyngburne
That I shal make , thyn hert for to morne
For wel I wot , thy pacience is a gon
¢ Oure host cried pees , and that a noon
And seide , late the womman telle hire tale
ñe fare as folk , that dronken ben of Ale
Do dame , telle fortú ©oure tale , & that is best

note.notetxt: 827There are two small and faint crosses in dry point in the left margin.ODR

folio.l: Al redy sire q¶ she , rigút as ©ow list
If I haue lycence , of this worthy Frere
ñis dame q¶ he , teÂ fortú , & I wyl here

note.notetxt: 830The size of the hand is smaller. The appearance of the handwriting is crammed. Was the space available not big enough? Was WBT already copyed? ODR

folio.l: ¢ Hic desinit prologus , et incipit fabula vxåis de Bathe
